items.title: Government responses criticised two months on from Kumanjayi Little Baby

children.content: Eight weeks on from the alleged murder of Kumanjayi Little Baby, Alice Springs community leaders are calling for an audit to determine why federal and territory government spending does not seem to be improving Indigenous living conditions and safety.

gallery.altText: A pink flower resting on the ground, colourful candles are burning nearby.

thumbnail.altText: A pink flower resting on the ground, colourful candles are burning nearby.

avatar.alt: Jane Bardon in a blazer looking at the camera.

items.title: $800k for mediation not spent prior to fatal stabbing

children.content: Mediation projects were a key part of the Barkly Regional Deal signed in 2019, but many locals are asking why the process seemed to have halted prior to the stabbing death of a young man amid unrest in Tennant Creek.

image.altText: A multi-colour flower memorial resting on the edge of a curb in front of a home, with orange paint.

items.title: Deputy coroner releases findings into teen's suicide

children.content: A 17-year-old school captain, who was dux of year 12 and a talented athlete, appeared to have everything ahead of him. Then he took his own life.

image.altText: A graphic of a white silhouette and a white spotlight behind him with a blue background sketch marks in it.

items.title: Final beanie festival a hit with crowds as $400k worth of caps sold

children.content: The 30th and final Alice Springs Beanie Festival has been a sellout, setting a new record of $400,000 worth of beanies sold in three days.

articles.title: In pictures: Alice Springs celebrates 30 years of unique craft festival

children.content: As the Alice Springs Beanie Festival celebrates its 30th and final year, we're exploring the weird and wonderful handmade creations — and the characters working hard behind the scenes — that have made the annual event so special.

image.altText: A man smiling at the camera, on his head is a handmade beanie featuring a knitted monkey holding a banana.

items.title: Two men arrested, denied bail after violent unrest in Central Australia

children.content: Two men have been arrested and denied bail after a violent disturbance in Yuendumu, in which multiple vehicles were allegedly set alight by armed offenders.

image.altText: Gold lettering on the side of a building spells out: Law Courts Alice Springs

items.title: The immersive Indigenous language class where English is left at the door

children.content: After learning from Native American Yuchi families, Pertame elders and young mothers in Alice Springs are raising babies in a total immersion Language Nest and hope to extend the model into primary school.

articles.title: Pauline Hanson clarifies she will not seek to cut paid parental leave

children.content: Pauline Hanson has clarified she would not seek to cut government-funded paid parental leave if One Nation were in power, after opening the door to a wind-back at her National Press Club address last week.

image.altText: A woman in a red, with red hair, standing in a corridor in front of TV cameras.

articles.title: Hanson says Ben Roberts-Smith should be welcome at War Memorial event

children.content: Federal parliament will adjourn early today so politicians can attend the official opening of a hall as part of the controversial $550 million extension of the War Memorial.

items.title: WiseTech shares rise as chair denies human trafficking reports

children.content: WiseTech regains some ground after Monday's 18 per cent plunge. The ASX is seesawing between gains and losses. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: The blurred side profile of a man walking past a screen that says "ASX welcomes WiseTech Global".

items.title: 'A little bit of whining' and other early takeaways from US–Iran talks

children.content: The US claims "great progress" has been made with Iran during the first substantial talks to take place under the new deal to pause the war, but there are already conflicting messages from Iran. Here's what we know about the results of the negotiations so far.

image.altText: JD Vance speaks at a podium.

articles.title: Messi's record-breaking World Cup goals make believers of us all

children.content: Lionel Messi now stands alone with a record 18 World Cup goals. And for those who witnessed it, it was a transcendent experience.

items.title: Reflecting pool to be drained again weeks after multi-million-dollar renovation

children.content: The reflecting pool on Washington's National Mall will have to be drained again after algae and peeling paint appeared weeks after it was renovated.

image.altText: Several people holding large poles with one person in the pool and several USA flags behind them.

items.title: Children die in hot car as Europe bakes

children.content: At least 18 people died in France, including two children left in a hot car, as a heatwave hung over Europe, smashing temperature records in several cities.

image.altText: People cool off in the Trocadero Fountain next to the Eiffel Tower in Paris.

items.title: Union launches bid to save Boag's brewery from closure

children.content: The United Workers Union has launched a petition calling on Boag's parent company Lion Australia to sell rather than close its Launceston brewery. The union has also written to Premier Jeremy Rockliff urging him to intervene.

image.altText: a red brick building with grey skies and green signage

items.title: Inspired by volcanoes, this new technology could affect billions of people

children.content: Private companies want to reflect the Sun to cool the planet. But scientists fear the geoengineering technology could disrupt monsoon rains, on which a billion people depend.

image.altText: the earth rotates and is surrounded by tiny reflective particles

items.title: You won't see Hugh Jackman the same after The Death of Robin Hood

children.content: Plus Toy Story 5 trots out the same old games, Australian horror scores another winner and Colony uses zombies to ring the warning bell about AI.

items.title: This artist 'stole' the Rosetta Stone from the UK and took it home to Egypt

children.content: The Rosetta Stone changed the way we understood Ancient Egypt. It sits in the British Museum but this artist wanted to return it home. To pull it off he needed some legal advice, a digital scanner and the courage to take on the way we look at history.

image.altText: A man stands wearing a full plastic mask with just eye holes, in the blue, white and red pattern of large plastic storage bags

items.title: More or Less quiz: Which British PMs have lasted the longest?

children.content: Some British PMs have held office across decades. Others have struggled to outlast a lettuce. It's time to test your knowledge.

image.altText: A screenshot from a Daily Star broadcast shows a lettuce with googly eyes next to portrait of Liz Truss

items.title: Egg shortage unlikely as poultry industry braces for deadly bird flu

children.content: The poultry industry says it is concerned but prepared as the deadly H5 avian influenza variant reaches Australian shores for the first time.

image.altText: Close of up a chicken's head.

items.title: The hidden dynamic that could undermine global stocks

children.content: As we plough on through the fourth consecutive year of global stock records and huge annual advances, it's not surprising that many pundits have begun to question just how much longer this boom can continue.

image.altText: A white rocket launches into the sky on a clear day, with grey and yellow clouds billowing below.

items.title: 'More bad news' expected as testing widens after second WA bird flu detection

children.content: Producers across an enormous stretch of southern WA are facing a tense few days, as authorities investigate more than 50 dead birds found along the state's southern coast.

items.title: 'We've been looked after': Hantavirus cruise passengers out of quarantine

children.content: Six passengers from Australia and NZ linked to the deadly MV Hondius hantavirus outbreak are on their way home after completing 42 days of quarantine in Perth and being cleared to leave.

image.altText: A front-on shot of a white van driving at night with headlights on and a police van to the right.

items.title: Logging company gives new answer to parliament over logs sent to Victoria

children.content: Sustainable Timber Tasmania has corrected the record after earlier stating that all logs from public native forests are processed in Tasmania.

image.altText: A semi-trailer loaded with logs in a traffic lane approaching a port. The large Spirit of Tasmania ship is in front of it.

items.title: Veterans paid extra $7m in abuse compensation after review

children.content: The Defence Force Ombudsman made the extra payments after a federal court case forced it to admit its reparation scheme didn't consider the ongoing impact of assaults.

image.altText: Australian Defence Force (ADF) soldiers stand on parade in Townsville.

items.title: Christian Brothers 'squib' on abuse compensation, lawyer says

children.content: The Catholic order says it will sell off its remaining assets to fund abuse compensation, but the money raised is expected to fall short of what is owed to victims.

image.altText: Cross on top of a church

items.title: Residents worried about dramatic increase to Menindee Lakes water release

children.content: Residents living along the Darling River are worried the decision to double water releases from the Menindee Lakes will remove the system's drought reserve. But authorities say it will help rebuild populations of Murray cod downstream.

gallery.altText: A sun setting behind clouds at Lake Menindee in outback NSW.

items.title: Blues dealt fresh blow with Mitchell ruled out of Origin III

children.content: After a demoralising defeat to the Maroons, the Blues learn Latrell Mitchell will be unavailable for selection in the series-deciding State of Origin III.

image.altText: Latrell Mitchell playing for NSW in the 2025 State of Origin series.

items.title: Ex-Wimbledon champ handed four-year suspension for refusing doping test

children.content: Markéta Vondroušová, who lifted the Wimbledon trophy in 2023, is given a "significant ban" by the International Tennis Integrity Agency.

image.altText: Marketa Vondrousova holds the Wimbledon trophy in 2023.

items.title: Farmers worried absentee landholders hindering pest control

children.content: Farmers says some absentee landholders, who do not live on the land full-time, are not pulling their weight when it comes to feral pest management.

image.altText: man leaning on back of ute tray, next to pile of netting.

items.title: Child protection advocates slam decision to scrap SA social worker registration scheme

children.content: Child protection advocates say the South Australian government has let down the state's most vulnerable people by axing a mandatory registration scheme for social workers, but those in the DV sector say the move could help alleviate workforce shortages.

image.altText: A close up of a woman with long grey hair, looking off camera

items.title: Calls to upgrade 'sketchy' Sturt Highway freight route

children.content: Eight councils are calling for urgent safety upgrades to the popular freight and travel route spanning three states.

items.title: Emergency alerts and warnings still lost on multicultural communities

children.content: Multicultural communities may not automatically understand the intent behind Australia's emergency messaging, but there are ways to fix this.

items.title: Regional vet shortages fuel push for student placement support

children.content: Veterinarians are calling for more government support to attract and retain professionals in rural Australia where it often takes more than a year to fill jobs.
